title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new brand of sustainable running shoes.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve just bought a pair of those 'Eco-Sprints'. They’re surprisingly comfortable for being made of recycled plastic. || Woman: [1]I found the sizing to be a bit inconsistent[/1], though. I usually take a six, but I had to go up to a seven-and-a-half to get them to fit. || Man: I had the same issue! It’s a bit of a nightmare if you’re ordering online. || Woman: [2]What I really liked, though, was the grip on the soles[/2]. I went for a run in the rain yesterday and didn't slip once. || Man: I agree. It’s a very safe shoe for wet weather. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a great product, I just wish they’d standardise their measurements a bit more.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve just bought a pair of those 'Eco-Sprints'. They’re surprisingly comfortable for being made of recycled plastic.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the sizing to be a bit inconsistent, though. I usually take a six, but I had to go up to a seven-and-a-half to get them to fit.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! It’s a bit of a nightmare if you’re ordering online.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: What I really liked, though, was the grip on the soles. I went for a run in the rain yesterday and didn't slip once.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a very safe shoe for wet weather.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great product, I just wish they’d standardise their measurements a bit more.

questions.question: What did the woman find frustrating about the shoes?

options.A: the high cost of the materials
options.B: the lack of accurate fit information

questions.explanation: She notes that the 'sizing is inconsistent' and she had to go up several sizes [1].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the shoes'
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'grip on the soles' and how safe they feel in 'wet weather' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a new professional networking platform.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, have you created a profile on 'Pro-Connect' yet? It’s meant to be the new industry standard. || Woman: [3]I suspect the algorithm is just pushing sponsored posts from big corporations rather than showing me relevant local jobs[/3]. || Man: It is a bit heavy on the advertising. I spend most of my time hiding ads. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that the 'endorsement' feature is being abused by people who’ve never even worked with me[/4], which makes the whole thing feel a bit fake. || Man: I agree. It’s lost its credibility if anyone can vouch for your skills without knowing you. || Woman: Exactly. It’s a nice idea in theory, but it’s becoming more of a popularity contest than a professional tool.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, have you created a profile on 'Pro-Connect' yet? It’s meant to be the new industry standard.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the algorithm is just pushing sponsored posts from big corporations rather than showing me relevant local jobs.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is a bit heavy on the advertising. I spend most of my time hiding ads.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that the 'endorsement' feature is being abused by people who’ve never even worked with me, which makes the whole thing feel a bit fake.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s lost its credibility if anyone can vouch for your skills without knowing you.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a nice idea in theory, but it’s becoming more of a popularity contest than a professional tool.

questions.question: The woman believes the platform is biased towards
questions.explanation: She suspects the algorithm is 'pushing sponsored posts from big corporations' [3].

questions.question: What is the speakers' main complaint about the endorsement system?

options.A: It is too difficult to receive feedback.
options.C: It is overly complicated to navigate.

questions.explanation: They agree it's 'fake' and 'lost its credibility' because strangers can endorse skills [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new local juice bar.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I went to 'The Squeeze' this morning. That ginger and kale shot really woke me up. || Woman: [5]I found the staff to be a bit overly pushy with their 'membership' scheme[/5], to be honest. I just wanted a drink, not a long-term commitment. || Man: I see your point. They are a bit eager to sign people up. || Woman: [6]I did appreciate the eco-friendly straws and cups, though[/6]. It’s good to see a business actually taking their waste seriously. || Man: I agree. It makes the slightly higher prices more acceptable. || Woman: Definitely. I’ll go back for the juice, I’ll just make sure to bring my noise-cancelling headphones to avoid the sales pitch.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I went to 'The Squeeze' this morning. That ginger and kale shot really woke me up.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the staff to be a bit overly pushy with their 'membership' scheme, to be honest. I just wanted a drink, not a long-term commitment.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I see your point. They are a bit eager to sign people up.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did appreciate the eco-friendly straws and cups, though. It’s good to see a business actually taking their waste seriously.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It makes the slightly higher prices more acceptable.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Definitely. I’ll go back for the juice, I’ll just make sure to bring my noise-cancelling headphones to avoid the sales pitch.

questions.question: What did the woman find annoying about her visit to the juice bar?

options.A: the slow service at the counter
options.B: the aggressive marketing of a loyalty program
options.C: the limited choice of healthy ingredients

questions.explanation: She describes the staff as 'overly pushy' regarding their 'membership scheme' [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the business's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'eco-friendly straws and cups' and the seriousness regarding waste [6].
